catalog.title: List all models
catalog.descriptionHTML: Get a list of models available for use, including details like supported input/output modalities, publisher, and rate limits.

example.name: OpenAI GPT-4.1
example.summary: gpt-4.1 outperforms gpt-4o across the board, with major gains in coding, instruction following, and long-context understanding

id.description: The unique identifier for the model

registry.description: The registry where the model is listed

summary.description: A brief summary of the model's capabilities

rate_limit_tier.description: The rate limit tier for the model

html_url.description: The URL to the model's detail page

capabilities.description: A list of capabilities supported by the model

limits.description: The limits for the model, including input/output token limits

max_input_tokens.description: The maximum number of input tokens allowed

max_output_tokens.description: The maximum number of output tokens allowed

tags.description: A list of tags associated with the model

supported_input_modalities.description: A list of input modalities supported by the model

items.description: An input modality supported by the model

supported_output_modalities.description: A list of output modalities supported by the model

items.description: An output modality supported by the model

embeddings.title: Run an embedding request attributed to an organization

parameters.name: api-version
parameters.description: The API version to use. Optional, but required for some features.

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization login associated with the organization to which the request is to be attributed.

bodyParameters.name: model
bodyParameters.description: ID of the specific model to use for the request. The model ID should be in the format of {publisher}/{model_name} where "openai/text-embedding-3-small" is an example of a model ID. You can find supported models in the catalog/models endpoint.

bodyParameters.name: input
bodyParameters.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of strings. To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass an array of strings. Each input must not exceed the max input tokens for the model, cannot be an empty string, and any array must be 2048 dimensions or less.

bodyParameters.name: encoding_format
bodyParameters.description: The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either float or base64.

bodyParameters.name: dimensions
bodyParameters.description: The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have. Only supported in text-embedding-3 and later models.

bodyParameters.name: user
bodyParameters.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help us to monitor and detect abuse.

embeddings.descriptionHTML: This endpoint allows you to run an embedding request attributed to a specific organization. You must be a member of the organization and have enabled models to use this endpoint. The token used to authenticate must have the models: read permission if using a fine-grained PAT or GitHub App minted token. The request body should contain the model ID and the input text(s) for the embedding request. The response will include the generated embeddings.

input: The food was delicious and the waiter was very friendly.
I had a great time at the restaurant.

schema.title: Embedding Response
schema.description: The response for an embedding request.

object.description: The object type, which is always "list" for embedding responses.

items.title: Embedding Object
items.description: An embedding vector returned by the model.

object.description: The object type, which is always "embedding".

index.description: The index of the embedding in the list of embeddings.

embedding.description: The embedding vector, which is a list of floats.

model.description: The model used for the embedding.

prompt_tokens.description: The number of tokens in the input.

total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens processed.

schema.title: Embedding Response
schema.description: The response for an embedding request.

object.description: The object type, which is always "list" for embedding responses.

items.title: Embedding Object

inference.title: Run an inference request attributed to an organization

parameters.name: api-version
parameters.description: The API version to use. Optional, but required for some features.

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization login associated with the organization to which the request is to be attributed.

bodyParameters.name: model
bodyParameters.description: ID of the specific model to use for the request. The model ID should be in the format of {publisher}/{model_name} where "openai/gpt-4.1" is an example of a model ID. You can find supported models in the catalog/models endpoint.

bodyParameters.name: messages
bodyParameters.description: The collection of context messages associated with this chat completion request. Typical usage begins with a chat message for the System role that provides instructions for the behavior of the assistant, followed by alternating messages between the User and Assistant roles.

childParamsGroups.name: role
childParamsGroups.description: The chat role associated with this message

bodyParameters.name: frequency_penalty
bodyParameters.description: A value that influences the probability of generated tokens appearing based on their cumulative frequency in generated text. Positive values will make tokens less likely to appear as their frequency increases and decrease the likelihood of the model repeating the same statements verbatim. Supported range is [-2, 2].

bodyParameters.name: max_tokens
bodyParameters.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate in the completion. The token count of your prompt plus max_tokens cannot exceed the model's context length. For example, if your prompt is 100 tokens and you set max_tokens to 50, the API will return a completion with a maximum of 50 tokens.

bodyParameters.name: modalities
bodyParameters.description: The modalities that the model is allowed to use for the chat completions response. The default modality is text. Indicating an unsupported modality combination results in a 422 error. Supported values are: text, audio

bodyParameters.name: presence_penalty
bodyParameters.description: A value that influences the probability of generated tokens appearing based on their existing presence in generated text. Positive values will make tokens less likely to appear when they already exist and increase the model's likelihood to output new tokens. Supported range is [-2, 2].

bodyParameters.name: response_format
bodyParameters.description: The desired format for the response.

bodyParameters.name: seed
bodyParameters.description: If specified, the system will make a best effort to sample deterministically such that repeated requests with the same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed.

bodyParameters.name: stream
bodyParameters.description: A value indicating whether chat completions should be streamed for this request.

bodyParameters.name: stream_options
bodyParameters.description: Whether to include usage information in the response. Requires stream to be set to true.

childParamsGroups.name: include_usage
childParamsGroups.description: Whether to include usage information in the response.

bodyParameters.name: stop
bodyParameters.description: A collection of textual sequences that will end completion generation.

bodyParameters.name: temperature
bodyParameters.description: The sampling temperature to use that controls the apparent creativity of generated completions. Higher values will make output more random while lower values will make results more focused and deterministic. It is not recommended to modify temperature and top_p for the same completion request as the interaction of these two settings is difficult to predict. Supported range is [0, 1]. Decimal values are supported.

bodyParameters.name: tool_choice
bodyParameters.description: If specified, the model will configure which of the provided tools it can use for the chat completions response.

bodyParameters.name: tools
bodyParameters.description: A list of tools the model may request to call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. The model may respond with a function call request and provide the input arguments in JSON format for that function.

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the function to be called.

childParamsGroups.name: description
childParamsGroups.description: A description of what the function does. The model will use this description when selecting the function and interpreting its parameters.

childParamsGroups.name: parameters
childParamsGroups.description: The parameters the function accepts, described as a JSON Schema object.

bodyParameters.name: top_p
bodyParameters.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature called nucleus sampling. This value causes the model to consider the results of tokens with the provided probability mass. As an example, a value of 0.15 will cause only the tokens comprising the top 15% of probability mass to be considered. It is not recommended to modify temperature and top_p for the same request as the interaction of these two settings is difficult to predict. Supported range is [0, 1]. Decimal values are supported.

inference.descriptionHTML: This endpoint allows you to run an inference request attributed to a specific organization. You must be a member of the organization and have enabled models to use this endpoint. The token used to authenticate must have the models: read permission if using a fine-grained PAT or GitHub App minted token. The request body should contain the model ID and the messages for the chat completion request. The response will include either a non-streaming or streaming response based on the request parameters.

messages.content: What is the capital of France?

message.content: The capital of France is Paris.

oneOf.title: Non Streaming Response
oneOf.description: A non-streaming response for the inference request.

message.description: The message associated with the completion.

oneOf.title: Streaming Response
oneOf.description: A streaming response for the inference request

delta.description: Container for the content of the streamed response.

content.description: The content of the streamed response.
